Anyone else experaning this?
No all ok for me
No issue for me
What browser and version of it are you using Tanya?
Im using chrome and let me check the version
Ok if it is V105.0 this is a recorded bug with a ticket in the backlog

Should we not raise an incident for this? I know I can update the version and it will stop effecting me.
But could this not be effecting customers?
Had a look at the market share of people using this version it is only at 0.09%
Still not sure if it should not be an incidnt. @gowthamkumar.b-dominos.co.uk What do you think?
@tanya, let see whehter all ar efacing same issue
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
if user affecting it is will be incident
is it with local issue with client?
We’ve had a couple of customers mention slow loading on Chrome
I don’t have numbers yet, but I can check reports
Could be just an intermittent issue
it is affecting the individual user we have check the client version
I’ve pulled some numbers — this is bigger than expected
About 20% of sessions are showing elevated load times
Seeing some timeouts as well
That’s much larger than Chrome 105’s share
@bob  pls confirm users are affecting
Could be tied to one of our deployments, perhaps

We made a few changes to the platform recently
https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/ZDQ1MTE3NWU5ZDYxNDA4MTkxZmM3ZGJmMGJhNmE1OTE

Title 
Slow load times for users using V105.0 of chrome
Description

User have reported the frontend as loading slowly and with quick look at there set up appears to be an issue with frontend for users using V105.0 of chrome.

User

OB-Frontend
Really don't think it's that serious, we know about this
This was raised 2 weeks ago
@daniel, CHG89553 these the deployment done on front end
@tanya @daniel replicate the issue
:star: Form an effective investigation team
:star: Reproduces the incident
If what Daniels says is true , none of today's deployments would be the cause
Unable to replicate this
Same
ok, check the logs which has latency and which service is taking more time to respond
:star: Facts over opinion
On it
If this is browser-specific and global, we should check proxy behavior
what will be the cause for slow down the web site
We made a change to Frontend around 2 weeks ago when the issue started
Could be a frontend performance regression
If rendering is slower for older Chrome, that would explain it
Maybe bundle size changes impacted legacy parsing
Investigating
If checkout forms are timing out, maybe retry logic is triggering delays.
or payment authorisation could be introducing wait states
change request CHG89462 is that causing issue
The delay appears before JS execution
That makes frontend less likely as root cause
Might be something happening at request handling stage
I am able to accessing website without latency
Todays changes don't line up with incident timeline
:star: Attempt to replicate the issue
Are you using chrome version 105?
i am creating incident
I’m checking NGINX logs for latency spikes by User-Agent
*Incident Number INC1015:* p2 latency accesing web site
:star: Incident record created
Really dont think we need an incident. As I said we have a bug already and really need to focus on delivery of new features.
@hamed can you pls check the network logs
Saw a ticket pop up, can we have more clarity of what seems to be an issue and impact?
Joining investigation
Seeing variation in response negotiation for older Chrome
yes, we create incident we have to quick to identify the issue before it affects the larger audience
Reviewing NGINX version history now
what will be the fix for the issue
@tanya @daniel what is the fix for it
Me and hamed are trying to pinpoint what is causing the delay time
Don't think this is application layer issue
There was a version upgrade around two weeks ago
ok then what will be the issue
That aligns more closely with impact timeline
We are investigating the possible causes, few minutes please
is the rollback of the CHG89462 will solve the issue
:star: Form a working theory
We can, but it most likeley will do nothing
Confirmed — NGINX version update introduced compatibility issue
Older Chrome versions are experiencing delayed request handling
Some headers/feature negotiation aren’t fully supported
ok, what we have to do in these case
roll back to previous version
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
We can either roll back NGINX or patch config
Rollback it is
ok, go ahade with patch
Working on it
ok please go for rollback it asap
This could have been caught earlier with better segmentation
:star: Take command
We need better early detection for edge-layer regressions
Hey @gowthamkumar.b-dominos.co.uk I have just retried the website with V105.0 of chrome and it seems the fix worked
Glad we created an incident for this. Need to review our training on when to create an incident or a bug but great work team.
Sorry guys should have raised an incident when first discovered.
We are all learning Daniel. We can carry the learnings foward
Just checked and it looks all good now team
Lets continue monitoring this and we can have a great conversation during the PIR
@bob any request coming
thank you team
